feed.title: RA: Resident Advisor Magazine
feed.subtitle: News, features, reviews and podcasts from RA

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: TREKKIE TRAX
entry.content: A high-energy mix of house and techno rollers from the Tokyo collective.

entry.title: Chicago's Signal Records expands to New York
entry.content: The store will open a branch in Brooklyn later this year.

entry.title: Spekki Webu - Bootstrap Paradox
entry.content: Bending techno into thrilling new shapes, the Dutch artist conjures a trippy, alien album that speaks directly to rave culture's foundational spirit.

entry.title: Arca announces new album, XXXXX
entry.content: The 16-track record is out on July 31st via XL Recordings.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Genoe
entry.content: A patient, near-ten hour mix through an ever-shifting musical landscape.

entry.title: Frankfurt's Module Festival confirms Suzanne Ciani, Skee Mask and upsammy for 2026 lineup
entry.content: The free event will take over Kulturcampus Frankfurt on August 13th through 16th.

entry.title: New experimental music club to open in Nijmegen
entry.content: Stichting RBBR is transforming a former EKI rubber factory into a music venue.

entry.title: Free open air events legalised in Berlin's Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg
entry.content: The district becomes the first in the city granted permission to legally host spontaneous, non-commercial events outdoors.

entry.title: Thessaloniki's Reworks Festival unveils first names for 2026
entry.content: Paul Kalkbrenner, Dixon, Fadi Mohem and Daria Kolosova will be heading to Greece this September.

entry.title: RA.1048 Traumer
entry.content: A masterclass in the art of the all-nighter, as one of France's finest purveyors of funk delivers six hours live from Robert Johnson.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Lawrence Lee & Nene H
entry.content: Multifaceted club tunes.

entry.title: A warehouse venue is opening in Leeds
entry.content: An opening date is yet to be announced.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Om Unit
entry.content: Tracing a forgotten thread between street soul and the hardcore continuum.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Silva Snipa
entry.content: Rollicking drum & bass rollers on wax.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: d.jezzz
entry.content: Laidback beats for a sunset wind-down.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Dexter Colt & Sunday Lee
entry.content: Deep dance cuts for mind, body and soul.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Gizmoe
entry.content: A genre-traversing session that reaffirms the dance floor's status as a place of protest.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Malibu & Crystallmess
entry.content: Suspended between dream and dissociation.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Icarus Redux
entry.content: Politically-charged soundscapes with Djrum-inspired scratching.

entry.title: New firm Miltone offers part-for-part recreation of the Oberheim Four Voice System
entry.content: The 4Exp can run as a standalone four-voice synth or expand an original FVS to eight voices.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: HeavenandHelen
entry.content: Buttery smooth Afro tech and deep house.

entry.title: London arts venue Theatreship to continue operating following new agreement
entry.content: Legal protections will be put in place to protect the space against future noise complaints.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Midirama & Raphael Kosmos
entry.content: A sprawling five-hour b2b recorded live at Fuchs2.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: pink.wav & Andreas Palmer
entry.content: Dubwise delights from the beloved Athenian DJs.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: OG Militant B
entry.content: A head-scrambling mix from the Tokyo wax specialist.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Marfox
entry.content: Rhythmic mastery, live from Ormside Projects.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Que DJ
entry.content: An hour-long gqom masterclass.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Felix Dickinson
entry.content: Groove-led selections, recorded on the terrace of Colombia's El Rio Hostel.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: A Lizard Called A
entry.content: A Munich mainstay in session.

entry.title: Positive Education returns from hiatus with K Wata, ABADIR, Manuka Honey
entry.content: Following a one-year break, the French gathering is back this November.

entry.title: This week's new music: Fundido, zayALLCAPS, bambinodj
entry.content: Welcome to our weekly music round-up. Every Friday afternoon, we'll share ten announcements from the last few days.

entry.title: The Cause adds Radio Alhara, Toumba, Kim Ann Foxman to festival lineup
entry.content: The Cause FM: New Frequency will be held at the East London venue on July 25th.

entry.title: fabric and Sennheiser to host Sven Väth at London's The Shard
entry.content: The Farringdon club and audio company are collaborating on a series of live events and activations.

entry.title: Korg releases NTS-4 six-channel performance mixer
entry.content: The latest addition to the Nu:Tekt series ships as a solder-free DIY kit.

entry.title: This week's top stories: RIP Lenzman, Boiler Room trampling, Divine Playhouse backlash
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: Various Artists - veneration for the sacred action
entry.content: Blurring rap, poetry and jazz, the London collective's debut compilation captures a bracing scene built on proximity and improvisation.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Travella
entry.content: Mix Of The Day: DJ Travella

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Azzi On The Beat & WEAREALLCHEMICALS
entry.content: Get to know mara, the Nigerian subgenre of Afrobeats.

entry.title: Arca announces takeover of worldwide radio stations
entry.content: NTS, Kiosk Radio, Dublab and others will simultaneously air a mix of the powerhouse artist's latest project.

entry.title: New York festival Sustain-Release reveals 2026 lineup
entry.content: DJ Nobu, livwutang, Verraco, james K and more will play the beloved festival in the Catskills this September.

entry.title: AIAIAI and Oklou unveil limited-edition headphone collaboration
entry.content: Custom versions of the Danish audio brand's TMA-2 and Tracks headphones will arrive this month.

entry.title: Listen back to three sets from AVA Belfast
entry.content: DJ Gigola, Pegassi and Holly Lester are among the first batch of mixes released from the festival.

entry.title: Tomorrowland fire reportedly ignited by ethanol leak
entry.content: The fire completely destroyed the festival's main stage ahead of its opening.

entry.title: Analogue Solutions releases limited edition monosynth
entry.content: Filtopia is a fully analogue desktop instrument capable of creating unusual formant sounds.

entry.title: New Manchester record shop opens in Northern Quarter
entry.content: Onsite Records, located on 81 Newton Street, opened on July 5th.

entry.title: New listening bar, Amber, opens in Vancouver
entry.content: The music-first space is located inside the Anchor Building on 99 Powell Street, Gastown.

entry.title: Ricardo Villalobos: Slipping Through Time
entry.content: Irrepressible, mystical, human: who else does it like Ricardo Villalobos? In this rare interview, our second cover story of summer 2026, Oli Warwick goes deep with the electronic iconoclast.

entry.title: Inside the Universe of When There Is No Sun
entry.content: The union of Sun Ra and Ricardo Villalobos was box office enough. But the story behind 2026's cosmic compilation runs far deeper. Here, key figures including Chez Damier, Calibre, Barış K and Villalobos discuss the project's voyage from Istanbul to Saturn and back.

entry.title: Sydney venue Divine Playhouse forced to close following backlash from Christian protesters
entry.content: The queer arts space recently took over a former church that had spent 70 years as a theatre.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Huey Mnemonic
entry.content: Jumping Detroit funk.

entry.title: Suno scraped songs from YouTube Music, Deezer, Genius, hacked data reveals
entry.content: New information from a 2025 hack sheds light on where the text-to-music tool allegedly scraped songs from.

entry.title: RA Pro Newsletter: Nina Protocol and the struggles of independence
entry.content: Reflections on the recent closure of the beloved music platform.

entry.title: San Francisco festival Entanglement announces lineup
entry.content: The collaboration between local promoter Endezeit and Detroit's Interdimensional Transmissions will feature Suzanne Ciani, Wata Igarashi, Clarisa Kimskii and more.

entry.title: Berlin club Kater launches reduced-price ticket for under-25s
entry.content: The €15 Next-Gen Ticket is available to clubbers aged 18 to 24.

entry.title: A new Actress album is on the way
entry.content: Radical Frame is out on September 18th via Ninja Tune.

entry.title: Logic1000 announces new album with Christine and the Queens, Jam City, FAUZIA
entry.content: Confirmation! is out on October 2nd.

entry.title: RA.1044 Love Injection
entry.content: A lesson in universal love, told via deep club grooves.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Bundang Rotary Cicadas
entry.content: Two hours of laidback house and downtempo.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Scott
entry.content: Rapid-fire makina from 2004.

entry.title: Change The Beat targets 300 label signings for women and gender-expansive artists by 2028
entry.content: The nonprofit has also announced a new initiative, The Demo Room, connecting emerging artists directly with labels and A&Rs.

entry.title: Playlist: Horst 2026
entry.content: Listen to 65 tracks that the RA team heard on the ground at this year's festival.

entry.title: Playlist: Draaimolen 2025
entry.content: Listen to 66 tracks heard by RA staff at the Dutch festival last weekend.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Ritornelo
entry.content: A Bogotá staple shows off his range.

entry.title: Playlist: Houghton 2025
entry.content: Listen to 110 tracks RA staff heard on the ground at the UK festival this year.

entry.title: Playlist: Horst Arts & Music 2025
entry.content: Listen to 85 tracks that defined this year's edition of the Brussels festival.

entry.title: Reptant - Ballet Robotique
entry.content: Ice-cold 808s from electro's new prince.

entry.title: Boards of Canada - Tape 05
entry.content: Warp's mysterious duo are back after 13 years. The surprise: it's some of their most beautiful work yet.

entry.title: fakemink - Terrified
entry.content: After crossing over on a wave of hype, the self-proclaimed saviour of the UK underground wastes his moment on a bloated album of trap, self-aggrandisement and poorly mixed electronics.

entry.title: Boards of Canada - Inferno
entry.content: Warp's IDM icons resurface after 13 years with a dark, disquieting record shaped by a violent world. Say goodbye to the fuzz and wistfulness.

entry.title: RHR - GÍRIA
entry.content: Thrilling baile funk fusions from one of São Paulo's most compelling club whisperers.

entry.title: Zora Jones - ANGEL CRISIS
entry.content: Zora Jones's first solo project in five years ushers in a new era with transformative, high-drama bass music.

entry.title: Excalibur and DJ Spence - Medium Rare
entry.content: Fatigued by the fast pace of algorithmic slop? Reach for this EP of unhurried perfection from one of minimal's best labels.

entry.title: Mala & Magugu - Militant Don
entry.content: Mala goes back to basics with heavyweight results.

entry.title: Beatrice M. - Midnight Swim
entry.content: Another hit from France's best hope for a modern day dubstep great.

entry.title: Protesters 'trampled' at Boiler Room New York
entry.content: Video footage shows an attendee jumping onto protesters during a "die-in" at the Under the K Bridge event. Boiler Room said it respects "the right to peaceful protest."

entry.title: Jasmín - Bite The Hand That Feeds You
entry.content: Hessle Audio's 50th release reflects on the label's techno-dubstep roots while barrelling bass-first into the future.

entry.title: Detroit festival Charivari unveils 2026 edition
entry.content: DJ Bone, DJ Spen, Eddie Fowlkes, LADYMONIX and more will play across seven events at both TV Lounge and Tangent Gallery.

entry.title: Munich club BLITZ unveils remaining July programme, closing party lineup
entry.content: The marathon weekender will feature Seth Troxler, Sam Goku, Ogazón, DVS1, HTRK and dozens more.

entry.title: Native Instruments announces collaboration with A.G. Cook
entry.content: Super*Saw compiles A.G. Cook's signature stacked saw patches to a single synthesiser.

entry.title: Answer Code Request - Halo
entry.content: The Berlin producer picks up where his 2018 LP Gens left off, wringing extreme feeling from techno's austere templates.

entry.title: New Pablo's Eye album to be released via STROOM
entry.content: Everything you give away is out on August 16th.

entry.title: Thaiboy Digital & swedm® - Paradise
entry.content: The Drain Gang mainstay's latest LP takes on big-room trance with aplomb.

entry.title: Four early Coil records to be remastered and reissued
entry.content: Gold Is The Metal (With The Broadest Shoulders), the first in the series, is out on October 30th.

entry.title: New community radio digital world map launched
entry.content: Community Radio Index is an interactive online tool that maps community radio stations across the globe.

entry.title: Christian protesters target new queer Sydney arts venue
entry.content: Divine Playhouse has opened in a former church that spent 70 years as a theatre.

entry.title: PinkPantheress to make acting debut in new film from 'The Daniels'
entry.content: The UK artist will join Matt Damon and Sandra Oh in the untitled Universal Pictures project.

entry.title: John Digweed to release live recording from Twilo reunion
entry.content: Live at Twilo was recorded earlier this year at the revival of the legendary New York club.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: jayah
entry.content: New-school Chicago club.

entry.title: Madrid club Sala Villanos unveils autumn programme
entry.content: Paquita Gordon, Kyle Hall and Sadar Bahar are among the upcoming bookings.

entry.title: Pennsylvania festival Honcho Campout reveals first names for 2026
entry.content: Mike Servito, Suze ijó, Sterling Juan Diaz and more will play the festival in August.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Cascou
entry.content: Acid folk, warm Balearic and soft-hued jazz-funk.

entry.title: Japanese festival Re:birth reveals first names for 2026
entry.content: The September event has booked Kuniyuki, Goth-Trad and Mathew Jonson b2b DJ Sodeyama.

entry.title: Munich's DNA Club announces closure
entry.content: "Thank you for three unforgettable years," the team shared in a statement.

entry.title: Hamburg festival Kampnagel Sommerfestival unveils 2026 programme
entry.content: The three-week event will bring dance, performance, music and visual art to venues across the city.

entry.title: Sydney's Club 77 to reopen this month
entry.content: A relaunch event with Rydeen and sovblkpssy will take place on July 30th.

entry.title: Brunch Electronik Lisbon unveils ten-year anniversary programme
entry.content: Adam Beyer, Charlotte De Witte and Solomun are heading to the Portuguese capital in the coming weeks.

entry.title: Erica Synths releases Razornator, a five-voice chromatic stereo resonator
entry.content: The FX box is Erica's fourth collaboration with Dutch plugin company 112dB.

entry.title: RA.1047 deep creep
entry.content: 77 minutes of wickedly twitchy grooves from the belly of the New York underground.

entry.title: Dutch drum & bass favourite Lenzman dies
entry.content: The North Quarter founder's family confirmed the news today.

entry.title: Akai releases new generation of its MPC One sampler
entry.content: The G2 features four times the processing power and twice the RAM.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Nick León
entry.content: Slow, sexy and sad jams that'll get you right in the feels.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Staryu
entry.content: A seamless survey of Thai grooves.

entry.title: Pete Tong denies copying John Summit's 'Missing' Remix
entry.content: The BBC Radio 1 presenter said the Franky Rizardo version was developed independently, while a separate proposed collaboration with Summit never progressed beyond initial discussions.

entry.title: Windy & Carl and Optigan Conservatory announce new collaborative album
entry.content: The record is out on September 4th via Kranky.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Jocelyn
entry.content: Polymorphous techno, bass and breaks recorded live at Berserker.

entry.title: This week's new music: Carmen Villain, Jay Mitta, re:ni
entry.content: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

entry.title: Bootleg KTV unveils new compilation of East and Southeast Asian queer dance music
entry.content: BKTV ALL-STARS COMP 001 is out now.

entry.title: This week's top stories: Andre King allegations, surprise Four Tet album, Pete Tong debacle
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Runner
entry.content: Classy 2-step UKG primed for summer.

entry.title: Four Tet releases surprise album as ⣎⡇ꉺლ༽இ•̛)ྀ◞ ༎ຶ ༽ৣৢ؞ৢ؞ؖ ꉺლ
entry.content: His first full-length album under the Wingdings alias is now available to stream.

entry.title: Charli xcx announces intimate shows in New York and London
entry.content: The UK artist will play at Music Hall of Williamsburg and The Scala in the coming days.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Givi Gelashvili
entry.content: Deep cuts from a Tbilisi veteran.

entry.title: Glasgow festival Sonica unveils 2026 lineup
entry.content: Suzanne Ciani, Loma Doom, DEBONAIR and Isa Gordon have been booked for the multi-venue September event.

entry.title: German festival Love Family Park shares archival sets from Ricardo Villalobos, Chris Liebing
entry.content: The LOST TAPES series has been launched to mark the Frankfurt weekender's 30th anniversary.

entry.title: RA to host Dekmantel production workshops with James Holden, Luke Slater, Olof Dreijer
entry.content: The Amsterdam festival's conference programme also includes a Q&A with Juan Atkins.

entry.title: Top Ten Festivals 2026: August
entry.content: Each month, we highlight ten of the best festivals happening around the world.

entry.title: London club FOLD unveils summer programming
entry.content: Key Vinyl, Ute.rec and Bassiani are set to host EXTENDED events.

entry.title: Franky Rizardo withdraws from Pete Tong's 'Missing' remix amid John Summit dispute
entry.content: The Dutch DJ said the track will remain a "set-only" exclusive after learning more about the circumstances surrounding its planned release.

entry.title: Berlin festival Wall2Wall announces 2026 lineup
entry.content: Erol Alkan, Monolake, Yanamaste and more will perform at the RSO event next month.

entry.title: Central Processing Unit releases spectral FX plugin
entry.content: CPU Blur was built on academic computer music tools using algorithms from the Composers Desktop Project.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: LUUTZZ
entry.content: Bouncing breaks.

entry.title: Film: The Sound of the 2020s Is…Nothing?
entry.content: No new sound has fully broken through in the 2020s. We explore why.

entry.title: A new club ORIGIN is opening in Los Angeles
entry.content: The Arts District space is set to open later this year.

entry.title: Music workers' union UMAW launches new campaign to improve artist treatment at venues
entry.content: Raise the Bar pushes venues to commit to a set of standards, including no merch cuts and pay-to-play.

entry.title: New book on classic albums features Richie Hawtin, Tricky, William Basinski
entry.content: Written by Emma Robertson, Breakthrough—20 Artists on the Album that Changed Everything also includes Max Richter, Laraaji and Peaches.

entry.title: Bay Area parties Parameter and SQUISH unveil lineup for weekender
entry.content: DjRUM, Skee Mask, Mala, Mark Ernestus, Verraco, Loidis will play the October event.

entry.title: Jen Cardini curates summer music series at Paris museum Palais de Tokyo
entry.content: Tokyo Waves will feature sets from BASHKKA, upsammy and Beatrice M. in the coming weeks.

entry.title: Minimal Audio announces new plugin designed in collaboration with EPROM
entry.content: The plugin features 60 of EPROM's custom-designed patches for the Current synthesiser.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Lazarusman
entry.content: The many shades of house, from gospel to Afro house and more.

entry.title: Dutch festival Draaimolen reveals 2026 lineup
entry.content: The Tilburg event will host Shygirl, Los Thuthanaka, Eris Drew, james K and more in September.

entry.title: Berlin label and event series Home Again announces tenth anniversary lineup
entry.content: Eris Drew, Solomun, Chris Stussy and more will take part in the 40-hour party.

entry.title: Kelela - new avatar
entry.content: On her masterful comeback, Kelela reconfigures shoegaze, IDM and R&B into a Black, femme sound of the future.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: NUSNOOM
entry.content: Techno rollers on Taiwan radio.

entry.title: DistroKid receives new majority investment from private equity firm
entry.content: The terms of CVC Capital's investment were not disclosed, but the company was last reported to be seeking a $2 billion sale.

entry.title: Jeff Mills: Seeing Is Believing
entry.content: What is techno? Is it a sound, style, way of life? And is it even in good health? In the first of our summer cover stories, Jeff Mills proposes a new answer to the age-old question.

entry.title: London venue The Lion & Lamb cuts ties with cofounder Andre King amid abuse allegations
entry.content: The Hoxton space has cancelled its upcoming programme in order to "work out the next steps effectively."

entry.title: Victoria festival Hopkins Creek unveils ten-year anniversary lineup
entry.content: Powder, Sleep D and Richard Sen are among the artists heading to Taungurung Country this November.

entry.title: Berlin ambient bar kwia confirms closing date
entry.content: The beloved listening space will close on July 25th.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: umru
entry.content: Hyperpop madness.

entry.title: RA.1043 Pretty Girl
entry.content: 90 minutes of wistful house from the talented Steel City Dance Discs affiliate, perfect for easing into the night or winding down after the party.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: OAARIKI
entry.content: Thrilling selections from all-Aotearoa artists.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Doc Scott & MC Justyce
entry.content: Archival drum & bass, recorded live at The Blue Note in 1997.

entry.title: Auckland club Neck of the Woods is no longer closing
entry.content: The venue raised nearly NZD $160,000 after the announcement of its closure.

entry.title: A new Venezuela earthquake relief compilation has been released
entry.content: The first volume of PULSO is out now, which will feature over 100 artists across three editions.

entry.title: Inside the world of sync: what music supervisors want from artists
entry.content: At last month's Doors Open event in East London, music supervisors, composers and agents shared practical advice on breaking into sync licensing.

entry.title: horsegiirL - Nature Is Healing
entry.content: The debut album from the world's first half-horse, half-human DJ is a unique accomplishment for both species.

entry.title: Prospa's Harvey Blumler 'to take some time out' following ear issues
entry.content: The Leeds artist suffered two perforated eardrums and an ear infection in recent weeks.

entry.title: Bake loses 'majority of current music' after bag stolen in London
entry.content: The Glasgow DJ was returning home after playing at Ormside Projects last Friday.

entry.title: RA.1046 Jennifer Loveless
entry.content: 90 minutes of scintillating house and techno, primed for the heart of summer.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Carolf
entry.content: Sleek dub techno from the Lisbon selector.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Caio Prince
entry.content: A set of funk bruxaria that goes the distance.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Westy b2b Omar
entry.content: A purpose-driven b2b linking Newcastle and Bethlehem.

entry.title: Montreal festival Ctrl+Alt announces programming for third edition
entry.content: Taking place from July 24th to 26th, the free festival will highlight local queer artists.

entry.title: London club fabric announces autumn and winter programme
entry.content: Ricardo Villalobos, Sama' Abdulhadi, Ogazón, tINI and dozens more will play between October and December.

entry.title: This week's new music: Marina Herlop, FaltyDL, Agazero
entry.content: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

entry.title: This week's top stories: Lukas Wigflex allegations, AlphaTheta unveils CDJ-1500X, Alex Jukes court hearing
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: Resident Advisor stage at Waterworks 2026 includes Eris Drew, OK Williams
entry.content: Now taking place at East London venue The Cause, the festival has also announced set times.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: ENTROPÍA
entry.content: Cumbia eclecticism.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Sewa Choki
entry.content: A comprehensive look at the Austin-based up-and-comer.

entry.title: Lukas Wigflex cancels upcoming shows following allegations by ex-wife and business partner
entry.content: Both parties shared new statements with Resident Advisor today. The Nottingham DJ has also stepped down from co-running UK festival Peep.

entry.title: Djrum announces new EP, I Wander
entry.content: The two-track release is out via Houndstooth on August 14th

entry.title: A History of Ghettotech in Ten Tracks
entry.content: Beloved by the likes of Zack Fox, MCR-T and SHERELLE, ghettotech is the ultimate floor-filler with its 808 architecture and attention-grabbing vocals. Learn about the quintessential Detroit sound via its pioneer, DJ Godfather.

entry.title: Listen back to 12 sets from Finland's Solstice Festival
entry.content: Mixes from Loidis, Kiernan Laveaux, livwutang and Verraco are available via The Lot Radio now.

entry.title: AlphaTheta unveils compact CDJ-1500X multi player with cloud support and CoBeat integration
entry.content: The new player borrows features from the flagship CDJ-3000X in a smaller format aimed at bars, small clubs and portable DJ setups.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Dresden
entry.content: A Glastonbury epic.

entry.title: Jukebox PR founder Alex Jukes faces Crown Court hearing for stalking and assault allegations
entry.content: The dance music podcaster and PR executive has pleaded not guilty to all charges.

entry.title: Luca Durán's new album features Debit, Gavsborg, Rosa Pistola
entry.content: Killing Mariposas is out on September 18th.

entry.title: Village people cofounder Victor Willis dies aged 74
entry.content: Willis died following a "short but aggressive illness," the group confirmed.

entry.title: Iceland Eclipse festival adds Tiga, Art Department for 2026
entry.content: The five-day evnet will take place on the Snæfellsnes Peninsula during the country's last total solar eclipse until 2196.

entry.title: LSO St Luke's, home of London Symphony Orchestra, opens new recording studio
entry.content: The launch is part of a multi-million-pound investment in the Old Street building's creative and technical facilities.

entry.title: Amsterdam Dance Event announces first 250-plus artists for 30th anniversary edition
entry.content: The annual festival and conference will host the likes of Avalon Emerson, DJ Nobu, Eris Drew and Skepta this October.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Neel, .VRIL, Mike Parker
entry.content: Five hours of live hardware jams.

entry.title: Body Hack organiser ARCHANGEL: 'It all takes work, time and collaboration'
entry.content: A crucial organiser in New York's queer underground is the subject of our next Underground Insider interview.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Auntie Chanel
entry.content: A taste of Detroit's queer underground.

entry.title: Ela Minus and Nick León announce collaborative EP
entry.content: qué les pasó a mis amigos? will be available August 7th, with a vinyl edition arriving in November.

entry.title: Play on New York rave scene to premiere at Edinburgh Fringe
entry.content: The Late Utopians debuts at Underbelly from August 5th.

entry.title: TIDAL introduces AI music labels and blocks royalties for wholly AI-generated tracks
entry.content: The streaming platform will begin tagging fully AI-generated music from mid-July.

entry.title: New music and film discovery platform Corus launches
entry.content: The social app combines music and film recommendations with community features, offering an alternative to algorithm-driven discovery.

entry.title: NANO Industries launches ISO-F1 analogue DJ performance isolator
entry.content: The Valencia-based company has unveiled a compact analogue isolator and filter unit for DJs and live performers.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Yuoto Saito
entry.content: Psychedelic dubstep and techno.

entry.title: Ottawa approves new annual authorisation for all-night dance events
entry.content: The motion allows venues to apply for annual licenses, instead of per-event, for all-night events.

entry.title: Dublin venue Yamamori Izakaya to cease club nights following dispute with The Hoxton
entry.content: The team said parties will halt from July 19th, but expressed excitement at the launch of a new venue.

entry.title: Listen back to six sets from Glastonbury's Block9 stage
entry.content: The recordings from last year's festival capture Dave Harvey, ISAbella, Guy Williams and others.

entry.title: Body of newborn baby found at Michigan festival Electric Forest
entry.content: Organisers said they're "heartbroken" at the discovery. Police continue to investigate.

entry.title: Rotterdam's Nous’klaer Festival cancels due to heat, launches fundraiser
entry.content: Organises cited the heatwave's "ongoing pressure on healthcare services" as the main reason for cancelling.

entry.title: PC Music to reissue early A. G. Cook and Danny L Harle project Dux Content
entry.content: Lifestyle, originally released in 2012, will be available on July 10th.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Maher Daniel
entry.content: A timeless festival set, recorded at Sunwaves in Bulgaria.

entry.title: RA.1045 Christian AB
entry.content: Two sublime hours of house and techno from the Berlin-based DJ and producer, making his long-awaited RA Mix debut.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Nixxy Rain
entry.content: Summery liquid and drum & bass.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Jll0Mll0
entry.content: Manic and abrasive experimentalism live from Oxford Art Factory.

entry.title: Nashville club Lulo Sound Room to close on July 1st
entry.content: The venue cited issues with the city and rent hikes as reasons behind the closure.

entry.title: This week's top stories: TILLATEC closure, AI Watchdog, new Berlin venue
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: Batu & Donato Dozzy - Exhale
entry.content: Two generational artists come together for an album of restrained, cerebral techno with a hint of bass weight.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Scotia
entry.content: Two hours of perfectly paced Detroit grooves.

entry.title: RA.1042 Soft Crash
entry.content: Phase Fatale and Pablo Bozzi shake loose the techno darkness for seven hours of camp, chug and genre-hopping hedonism, recorded live at Kyiv's ∄.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Dreams
entry.content: Two hours of diverse electronic music from one of the year's best compilations.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: akii
entry.content: Expansive dubwise selections from the Tokyo-based DJ.

entry.title: New venue FOUND launches in Berlin with Watergate open-air event
entry.content: The Tempelhof-Schöneberg space was the home of KitKatClub from 2001 until 2004.

entry.title: Polish festival Unsound unveils first acts for 2026
entry.content: Actress, Tim Hecker and PPP are among the dozens playing the festival in October.

entry.title: Dean Blunt unveils surprise compilation project
entry.content: The unnamed 13-minute release compiles unreleased material from 2022 to 2025.
